<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace delete_inactive_users {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> last_login;
  std::string status = "active";
};

int delete_inactive_users() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    sqlgen::Session session(conn);

    session.start_write();

    const auto delete_stmt = session.prepare_delete<User>().where([](const auto& _user) {
      return _user.last_login < sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 1, 1);
    });

    const auto res = delete_stmt.execute();
    if (!res) {
      session.rollback();
      return 1;
    }

    return session.commit() ? 0 : 1;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return 1;
  }
}
} // namespace delete_inactive_users
```</answer>

      rfl::NamedTuple<CID=rfl::Int, CName=rfl::String, Term=rfl::Int<1>,
                        CTime=rfl::Optional<int>, Credit=rfl::Float<0.0>];

  static constexpr bool has_primary_key = true;
  static constexpr bool primary_key_is_composite = false;

  template <class OtherType>
  friend consteval auto operator||(
      const Course&,
      const rfl::internal::TypeHolder<OtherType>&) noexcept {
    return OtherType{};
  }
};

struct Score {
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<Student, "SNo"> SNo;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<Course, "CID"> CID;
  sqlgen::Column<std::optional<float>> Score;

  using ReflectionType =
      rfl::NamedTuple<SNo=rfl::String, CID=rfl::Int, Score=rfl::Optional<float>>;

  static constexpr bool has_primary_key = true;
  static constexpr bool primary_key_is_composite = true;

  template <class OtherType>
  friend consteval auto operator||(
      const Score&,
      const rfl::internal::TypeHolder<OtherType>&) noexcept {
    return OtherType{};
  }
};

void design_eas_database() {
  const auto file = std::string("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto uri = sqlgen::sqlite::make_uri(file);
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(uri);

  const auto create_student = Student{}.to_sql_create_table();
  const auto create_course = Course{}.to_sql_create_table();
  const auto create_score = Score{}.to_sql_create_table();

  conn->execute(create_student);
  conn->execute(create_course);
  conn->execute(create_score);
}

}  // namespace test_design_eas_database

#endif

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_active_users_posts.hpp"

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create User table
  const auto user_create_stmt = User::create_as();
  conn.execute(user_create_stmt);

  // Create Post table
  const auto post_create_stmt = Post::create_as();
  conn.execute(post_create_stmt);

  // Build the query
  const auto query = sqlgen::QueryBuilder<ActiveUser>()
      .from<User>("u")
      .join<Post>("p", "u.user_id = p.user_id")
      .where("p.created_at >= DATE('2025-12-01') AND p.created_at <= DATE('2025-12-31')")
      .select("u.user_id", "u.username")
      .distinct()
      .build();

  // Execute the query and fetch results
  return conn.execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& _it) {
        std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
        for (; _it; ++_it) {
          result.emplace_back(std::move(*_it));
        }
        return result;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<ActiveUser>());
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "sqlgen.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.h"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/Connection.h"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> course_id;
  std::string title;
};

struct Enrollment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
  std::optional<float> grade;
};

struct CourseStats {
  std::string title;
  int student_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_grade;
};

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

    const auto create_course_table = sqlgen::write_schema<Course>();
    conn->execute(create_course_table);

    const auto create_enrollment_table = sqlgen::write_schema<Enrollment>();
    conn->execute(create_enrollment_table);

    const std::string query =
        "SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) AS student_count, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade "
        "FROM Course c "
        "LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id "
        "GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;";

    const auto res = conn->execute(query);

    const auto to_course_stats = [](const auto& _row) -> CourseStats {
      return CourseStats{.title = std::move(_row[0].get<std::string>()),
                         .student_count = _row[1].get<int>(),
                         .avg_grade = _row[2].transform([](const auto& _v) { return static_cast<double>(_v); })};
    };

    return sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(res | transform(to_course_stats));
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```</answer>

<answer>```python
import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SQLiteConnection

class Product(sqlgen.Table):
    product_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    category = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.String)

class Sale(sqlgen.Table):
    sale_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    product_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer)
    quantity = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer)
    amount = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Float)

class CategoryReport:
    def __init__(self, category, total_qty, total_amt):
        self.category = category
        self.total_qty = total_qty
        self.total_amt = total_amt

def query_product_sales_report():
    conn = SQLiteConnection("test_db.sqlite")
    query = (
        sqlgen.select_from(Product, [Product.category])
        .join(Sale, on=Product.product_id == Sale.product_id)
        .group_by(Product.category)
        .agg(total_qty=sqlgen.Sum(Sale.quantity), total_amt=sqlgen.Sum(Sale.amount))
        .having(sqlgen.Col("total_amt") > 5000)
    )
    results = conn.execute(query).fetchall()
    reports = []
    for row in results:
        reports.append(CategoryReport(row['category'], row['total_qty'], row['total_amt']))
    return reports
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "query_search_books_by_criteria.hpp"

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>().where(true);
  const auto& book = query.get_table_tuple_t().get<0>());

  if (filters.title_keyword) {
    query = query.where(book.title.like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%"));
  }

  if (filters.min_year) {
    query = query.where(book.year >= *filters.min_year);
  }

  if (filters.genre) {
    query = query.where(book.genre == *filters.genre);
  }

  if (filters.max_price) {
    query = query.where(book.price <= *filters.max_price);
  }

  auto res = conn.execute(query);

  std::vector<Book> books;
  res >> books;

  return books;
}
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct AuditLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> log_id;
  std::string table_name;
  int record_id;
  std::string action;
  sqlgen::DateTime timestamp;
};

bool create_trigger_audit_log() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn) {
      return false;
    }

    const std::string trigger_sql = R"(
      CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS audit_product_update
      AFTER UPDATE ON Product
      BEGIN
          INSERT INTO AuditLog (table_name, record_id, action, timestamp)
          VALUES ('Product', OLD.product_id, 'UPDATE', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
      END;
    )";

    return conn->execute(trigger_sql)
        .transform([](const auto&) { return true; })
        .value_or(false);
  } catch (...) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string sku;
  std::string name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

bool manage_indexes() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

    auto table_exists = conn.execute("SELECT count(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='Product'");

      conn.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_product_category ON Product(category)");
    }

    // Create idx_product_sku (unique)
    auto idx_sku = conn.execute("SELECT count(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_product_sku'");
    if (idx_sku.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0) {
      conn.execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_product_sku ON Product(sku)");
    }

    // Create idx_product_name_price
    auto idx_name_price = conn.execute("SELECT count(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_product_name_price'");
    if (idx_name_price.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0) {
      conn.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_product_name_price ON Product(name, price)");
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes

#endif

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <rfl.hpp>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct UserAfter {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string phone_number;
  bool is_active;
};

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    auto res = conn.execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
    if (!res) {
      return false;
    }

    std::unordered_set<std::string> existing_columns;
    for (const auto& row : res->rows()) {
      existing_columns.insert(row["name"]);
    }

    const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::string>> columns_to_add = {
        {"phone_number", "VARCHAR(20)"},
        {"is_active", "INTEGER DEFAULT 1"}
    };

    for (const auto& [col_name, col_type] : columns_to_add) {
      if (existing_columns.count(col_name)) {
        continue;
      }

      std::string sql = "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN " + col_name + " " + col_type + ";";
      try {

      } catch (const std::exception& e) {

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

struct Account {

}

  return pending_rows + shipped_rows;
}

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace update_order_status {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string status;
  sqlgen::DateTime order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

int update_order_status() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto tx = conn->begin_transaction();

  // Update pending orders older than 7 days to expired
  const auto pending_stmt = R"(
    UPDATE "Order"
    SET status = 'expired'
    WHERE status = 'pending'
      AND order_date < DATE('now', '-7 days')
  )";
  const auto pending_res = conn->execute(pending_stmt);
  const auto pending_rows = pending_res.rows_affected();

  // Update shipped orders to delivered (simulated delivery confirmation)
  const auto shipped_stmt = R"(
    UPDATE "Order"
    SET status = 'delivered'
    WHERE status = 'shipped'
  )";
  const auto shipped_res = conn->execute(shipped_stmt);
  const auto shipped_rows = shipped_res.rows_affected();

  tx.commit();
  return pending_rows + shipped_rows;
}

} // namespace update_order_status

<answer>
```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace upsert_user_profile {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

bool upsert_user_profile(const UserProfile& u) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string sql = "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO UserProfile (user_id, bio, avatar_url) VALUES (?, ?, ?);";
    auto stmt = conn->prepare(sql);
    stmt->bind(u.user_id.value());
    stmt->bind(u.bio);
    stmt->bind(u.avatar_url);
    stmt->execute();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

} // namespace upsert_user_profile
```
</answer>

#include <rfl.hpp>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {

};

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string query =
        "CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW SalesSummary AS "
        "SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(s.amount) AS total_revenue "
        "FROM Sale s "
        "JOIN Product p ON s.product_id = p.product_id "
        "GROUP BY p.name;";
    return conn->execute(query);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace right_join_departments_projects {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct DepProj {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::string project_name;
};

std::vector<DepProj> right_join_departments_projects() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = session->new_query()
      .select_from("Project", "p")
      .fields("d.name AS department_name", "COALESCE(p.name, '') AS project_name")
      .join("Department", "d", "p.department_id = d.department_id", "RIGHT")
      .where("");

  return session->template read<std::vector<DepProj>>(query.str());
}
}  // namespace right_join_departments_projects

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  const auto A_ = sqlgen::make_table_alias<A>();
  const auto B_ = sqlgen::make_table_alias<B>();

  const auto query = sqlgen::SelectFrom<A, B>{
      .fields = {A_.name_, B_.score_},
      .from = A_,
      .joins = {sqlgen::Join{.how = "INNER", .table_or_query = B_, .on = A_.id == B_.a_id}},
      .where = (B_.score > 10) && (A_.name.like("%x%")),
      .order_by = sqlgen::OrderBy{.columns = {B_.score.desc()}},
      .limit = 5
  };

  return conn.to_sql(query);
}
}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data(const std::vector<Event>& events) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->insert_all(events);

    const auto query = conn->select_from<Event>()
        .where(Event::end_time.is_not_null())
        .fields(
            Event::event_id,
            Event::event_name,
            sqlgen::functions::day(Event::start_time).as("start_day"),
            sqlgen::functions::month(Event::start_time).as("start_month"),
            sqlgen::functions::year(Event::start_time).as("start_year"),
            sqlgen::functions::hour(Event::start_time).as("start_hour"),
            sqlgen::functions::minute(Event::start_time).as("start_minute"),
            sqlgen::functions::second(Event::start_time).as("start_second"),
            sqlgen::functions::weekday(Event::start_time).as("start_weekday"),
            sqlgen::functions::unixepoch(Event::start_time).as("start_epoch"),
            sqlgen::functions::days_between(Event::start_time, Event::end_time).as("duration_days")
        );

    const auto result = query.transform([](auto&& _data) {
      return _data.to_container<std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>>();
    });

    return result.value_or(std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>());
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data
```

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <type_traits>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
};

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& _items) {
  try {
    // Connect to the SQLite database.
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

    // Begin a write session.
    auto write_session = conn.begin_write();

    // Perform upsert for all items.
    write_session->upsert(_items);

    // End the write session.
    write_session->end_write();

    // Begin a read session to verify.
    auto read_session = conn.begin_read();

    // Select all items from the Inventory table.
    const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Inventory>();
    auto db_items = read_session->template read<std::vector<Inventory>>(query);

    // Close the read session.
    read_session->end_read();

    // Create a map of item_id to Inventory for input and database.
    std::map<int, Inventory> input_map;
    for (const auto& item : _items) {
      input_map[item.item_id] = item;
    }

    std::map<int, Inventory> db_map;
    for (const auto& item : db_items) {
      db_map[item.item_id] = item;
    }

    // Check if the maps are equal.
    return input_map == db_map;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (e.g., log the error message).
    std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

#endif

import sqlgen
from datetime import datetime

def analyze_social_network():
    # Connect to the SQLite database
    conn = sqlgen.connect('sqlite:///test_db.sqlite')
    User = sqlgen.Table('User', columns={
        'user_id': sqlgen.PrimaryKey(int, auto_incr=True),
        'username': sqlgen.Column(str, unique=True, nullable=False),
        'email': sqlgen.Column(str, unique=True, nullable=False),
        'join_date': sqlgen.Column(datetime, nullable=False),
        'city': sqlgen.Column(str, nullable=True)
    })
    Friendship = sqlgen.Table('Friendship', columns={
        'user_id1': sqlgen.ForeignKey(User.user_id, nullable=False),
        'user_id2': sqlgen.ForeignKey(User.user_id, nullable=False),
        'friendship_date': sqlgen.Column(datetime, nullable=False)
    }, primary_keys=['user_id1', 'user_id2'])

    # Query 1: Mutual friends (friends who share the same friend)
    mutual_friends_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;
    """
    mutual_results = conn.execute(mutual_friends_query).fetchall()

    # Query 3: Friend-of-friend relationships
    friend_of_friend_query = """
    SELECT u1.username AS user1, u3.username AS user2
    FROM Friendship f1
    JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    JOIN User u1 ON f1.user_id1 = u1.user_id
    JOIN User u3 ON f2.user_id2 = u3.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE (f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u3.user_id)
        OR (f3.user_id1 = u3.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u3.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f1.user_id2) = 1;
    """
    foaf_results = conn.execute(friend_of_friend_query).fetchall()

    # Query 5: Same city non-friends
    same_city_non_friends_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f 
        WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
        OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    )
    """
    same_city_results = conn.execute(same_city_non_friends_query).fetchall()

    # Combine all results into FriendConnection objects
    results = []
    for res in mutual_results + foaf_results + same_city_results:
        results.append(FriendConnection(user1_name=res[0], user2_name=res[1]))

    return results

So, the code would look something like this:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct MathAnalysis {
  // ... fields as defined
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto rows = conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& res) { return res.rows; });

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> result;
  for (const auto& row : rows.value()) {
    MathAnalysis ma;
    ma.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    ma.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    ma.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    ma.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    ma.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    ma.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    ma.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    ma.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    ma.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    result.push_back(std::move(ma));
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT user_id,
           TRIM(name) AS trimmed_name,
           LENGTH(TRIM(name)) AS name_length,
           TRIM(email) AS trimmed_email,
           LENGTH(TRIM(email)) AS email_length
    FROM UserProfile;
  )";
  return conn->read<ValidatedProfile>(query);
}
}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

struct OrderMetrics {
  int item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;
  double final_total;
  double unit_cost;
  int quantity_group;
  double estimated_profit;
};

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(
    const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(":memory:");

    const std::string create_table_sql =
        "CREATE TABLE OrderItem (
            item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            unit_price REAL NOT NULL,
            quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
            discount_rate REAL,
            tax_rate REAL
        );";
    conn->execute(create_table_sql);

    for (const auto& item : items) {
      conn->insert(item);
    }

    const std::string select_sql =
        "SELECT 
          item_id,
          order_id,
          product_id,
          unit_price,
          quantity,
          COALESCE(discount_rate, 0) as discount_rate,
          COALESCE(tax_rate, 0) as tax_rate,
          (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
          (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100)) as discounted_total,
          (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0)/100)) as final_total,
          (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
          (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
          (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
        FROM OrderItem;";

    auto res = conn->execute(select_sql);

    std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics_list;

    while (res->next()) {
      OrderMetrics metrics;
      metrics.item_id = res->get<int>("item_id");
      metrics.order_id = res->get<int>("order_id");
      metrics.product_id = res->get<int>("product_id");
      metrics.unit_price = res->get<double>("unit_price");
      metrics.quantity = res->get<int>("quantity");
      metrics.discount_rate = res->get<double>("discount_rate");
      metrics.tax_rate = res->get<double>("tax_rate");
      metrics.gross_total = res->get<double>("gross_total");
      metrics.discounted_total = res->get<double>("discounted_total");
      metrics.final_total = res->get<double>("final_total");
      metrics.unit_cost = res->get<double>("unit_cost");
      metrics.quantity_group = res->get<int>("quantity_group");
      metrics.estimated_profit = res->get<double>("estimated_profit");
      metrics_list.push_back(std::move(metrics));
    }

    return metrics_list;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const std::string sql = "SELECT * FROM Customer WHERE customer_id = ?";
  auto prepared_stmt = conn->prepare(sql).value();
  prepared_stmt.bind_int(1, id);
  auto cursor = prepared_stmt.execute().transform([](auto&& _stmt) { return _stmt.cursor(); });
  std::vector<Customer> customers;
  cursor->read(customers);
  if (!customers.empty()) {
    return customers[0];
  } else {
    return Customer{}; // Return default if no record found
  }
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConnection("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = sqlgen::Session<sqlgen::SQLiteConnection>(conn);

  // Step 1: Read all notes
  std::vector<Note> notes;
  session.select_from<Note>().to(notes);

  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    session.update<Note>().set(note).where(note_id == note.note_id).execute();
  }

  // Step 4: Fetch updated notes
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  session.select_from<Note>().transform([](auto&& note) {
    return UpdatedNote{.note_id = note.note_id, .updated_content = note.content};
  }).to(result);

  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConnection("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = sqlgen::Session<sqlgen::SQLiteConnection>(conn);

  // Retrieve all notes from the database
  std::vector<Note> notes;
  session.select_from<Note>().to(notes);

  // Replace "foo" with "bar" in each note's content
  for (auto& note : notes) {
    size_t pos = 0;
    while ((pos = note.content.find("foo", pos)) != std::string::npos) {
      note.content.replace(pos, 3, "bar");
      pos += 3; // Move past the replaced substring
    }
  }

  // Update the database with modified content
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    session.update<Note>().set(note).where(note.note_id == note.note_id).execute();
  }

  // Fetch updated notes to return
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  session.select_from<Note>().transform([](auto&& note) {
    return UpdatedNote{.note_id = note.note_id, .updated_content = note.content};
  }).to(updated_notes);

  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

  }
}
}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace cascade_delete_demo {

struct Parent {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> parent_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Child {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> child_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Parent, "parent_id"> parent_id;
  std::string note;
};

bool cascade_delete_demo() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConn::make("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->createTable<Parent>();
    conn->createTable<Child>();

    // Insert parent
    Parent parent{.parent_id = 1, .name = "Parent1"};
    conn->insert(parent);

    // Insert child
    Child child{.child_id = 1, .parent_id = 1, .note = "Test note"};
    conn->insert(child);

    // Delete parent
    conn->execute("DELETE FROM Parent WHERE parent_id = 1;");

    // Check if child was deleted
    auto children = conn->template selectFrom<Child>("WHERE parent_id = 1");
    return children.empty();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace cascade_delete_demo
```
</answer>

struct Account {

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace update_set_operations {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
  std::string status;
};

bool update_set_operations() {
  try {
    auto connector = sqlgen::SQLiteConnector("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = connector.connect();

    // Create the Account table
    session.createAs<Account>().execute();

    // Insert a test account
    Account test_account{.account_id = 1, .balance = 100.0, .status = "active"};
    session.insert(test_account).execute();

    // Update balance and status using SET operations
    session.update<Account>()
        .where([](const auto& a) { return a.account_id == 1; })
        .set([](auto& a) { a.balance = 200.0; })
        .set([](auto& a) { a.status = "inactive"; })
        .execute();

    // Verify the update
    auto result = session.selectFrom<Account>().where([](const auto& a) { return a.account_id == 1; }).execute();
    if (!result || result->empty()) {
      return false;
    }
    const auto& updated_account = result->at(0);
    return updated_account.balance == 200.0 && updated_account.status == "inactive";
  } catch (const std::exception&) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace update_set_operations
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  try {
    sqlgen::SQLiteConnector connector;
    auto session = connector.connect("test_db.sqlite");

    auto query = session.select_from<Supplier>()
        .join<Product, sqlgen::JoinType::FullOuterJoin, [](const auto& s, const auto& p) {
          return s.supplier_id == p.supplier_id;
        })
        .fields("s.name", "p.name");

    auto result = session.execute(query).transform([](auto&& res) -> std::vector<SupProd> {
      std::vector<SupProd> vec;
      for (const auto& row : res) {
        SupProd sp;
        sp.supplier_name = row.get<std::string>("s.name");
        sp.product_name = row.get<std::optional<std::string>>("p.name");
        vec.push_back(std::move(sp));
      }
      return vec;
    });

    return result.value_or(std::vector<SupProd>());
  } catch (...) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products
```
</answer>

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace select_from_read_to_vector {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  int pages;
};

std::vector<Book> select_from_read_to_vector() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("books.db");
  if (!conn) {
    return {};
  }
  const auto query = "SELECT * FROM Book ORDER BY pages DESC";
  return conn->select_from<Book>(query)
      .transform([](auto&& r) { return r.to<std::vector<Book>>(); })
      .value_or(std::vector<Book>());
}

}  // namespace select_from_read_to_vector

namespace analyze_department_employees {

  std::optional<std::string> first_name;

};

  analysis.right_join_results = std::move(right_results);

  // Full Join

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>
#include <string>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
  JoinAnalysis analysis;

  // INNER JOIN
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_results;
  conn->read_to(inner_results,
      "SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary "
      "FROM Department d "
      "INNER JOIN Employee e ON d.department_id = e.department_id;");
  analysis.inner_join_results = std::move(inner_results);

  // LEFT JOIN
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_results;
  conn->read_to(left_results,
      "SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary "
      "FROM Employee e "
      "LEFT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;");
  analysis.left_join_results = std::move(left_results);

  // RIGHT JOIN
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_results;
  conn->read_to(right_results,
      "SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary "
      "FROM Employee e "
      "RIGHT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;");
  analysis.right_join_results = std::move(right_results);

  // FULL JOIN (emulated using UNION of LEFT and RIGHT JOIN)
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_results;
  conn->read_to(full_results,
      "SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary "
      "FROM Department d "
      "LEFT JOIN Employee e ON d.department_id = e.department_id "
      "UNION "

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;
  std::string status;
};

inline std::string delete_with_complex_condition() {
    auto query = sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where((age < 18) || (status == "inactive"));
    return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition
```
</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to write a C++ function using the sqlgen library to get the top-selling products from an SQLite database. Let's start by understanding the requirements.

First, the function is called get_top_products and takes a limit_count parameter (default 10). It should connect to test_db.sqlite. The goal is to join the Product and Sales tables, calculate total sales (price * quantity) per product, sort them in descending order, apply the limit, and return the results as a vector of TopProduct structs.

Looking at the provided SQL query, it uses INNER JOIN, SUM for total_sales, GROUP BY, ORDER BY, and LIMIT. So the task is to translate this into sqlgen's C++ API.

The Product struct has product_id as a PrimaryKey<int>, which should map to the product_id in the Product table. Similarly, Sales has sale_id as PrimaryKey<int>, product_id, quantity, and sale_date. The TopProduct struct holds the product details and total_sales.

In sqlgen, to perform a join, I can use the join method on a SelectFrom query. The query needs to select from Product (aliased as p) and Sales (s), join on p.product_id = s.product_id. Then calculate the sum of p.price * s.quantity as total_sales. Group by the product fields since they are non-numeric. Order by total_sales descending and limit the results.

The steps for the function:

1. Connect to the SQLite database using sqlgen::SQLiteConn.
2. Create a SelectFrom query joining Product and Sales.
3. Use aggregate functions: sum(p.price * s.quantity) as total_sales.
4. Group by product_id, product_name, category.
5. Order by total_sales DESC.
6. Apply LIMIT clause with the given limit_count.
7. Execute the query and map the results to TopProduct structs.

Potential issues: Making sure the columns are correctly selected and grouped. Also, handling the connection properly, maybe using a context or ensuring it's closed after use.

Now, translating the SQL into sqlgen code. The SelectFrom would be something like:

                   .where(...) if needed, but the reference SQL doesn't have a where clause.
Then select the fields: p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, sum(p.price * s.quantity) as total_sales.
Group by the product fields. Order by total_sales desc. Limit(limit_count).

But in sqlgen, when you do a join, you might need to alias the tables. Alternatively, use the join condition properly.

Wait, the Product struct's product_id is the primary key, and Sales has product_id as a foreign key. So the join condition is Product.product_id = Sales.product_id.

In code:

auto query = session.selectFrom<Product>("p")
                  .join<Sales>("s", "p.product_id = s.product_id")
                  .where(...) // not needed here
                  .select("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category", "SUM(p.price * s.quantity) AS total_sales")
                  .groupBy("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category")
                  .orderBy("total_sales DESC")
                  .limit(limit_count);

Then execute the query and process the results.

But how to handle the aggregation in the SelectFrom? The select method can include the SUM expression. Then groupBy the necessary columns.

Once the query is built, executing it would return a vector of tuples, which need to be mapped to TopProduct structs.

So in code:

auto result = query.execute(conn);

Then loop through each row in result, extract the values, and assign to TopProduct.

Each row would be a tuple with product_id (int), product_name (string), category (string), total_sales (double). So for each row in result.rows:

tp.product_id = row[0];
tp.product_name = row[1];
tp.category = row[2];
tp.total_sales = row[3];

Putting it all together:

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  sqlgen::SQLiteConn conn("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = conn.selectFrom<Product>("p")
    .join<Sales>("s", "p.product_id = s.product_id")
    .select("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category", "SUM(p.price * s.quantity) AS total_sales")
    .groupBy("p.product_id", "p.product_name", "p.category")
    .orderBy("total_sales DESC")
    .limit(limit_count);
  
  auto result = query.execute();
  std::vector<TopProduct> top_products;
  for (const auto& row : result.rows) {
    TopProduct tp;

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"

namespace get_top_products {

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  const auto to_top_product = [](const auto& _row) -> TopProduct {
    return TopProduct{
        .product_id = _row[0].as<int>(),
        .product_name = _row[1].as<std::string>(),
        .category = _row[2].as<std::string>(),
        .total_sales = _row[3].as<double>()};
  };

  const auto select_from = R"(
    SELECT 
      p.product_id,
      p.product_name,
      p.category,
      SUM(p.price * s.quantity) as total_sales
    FROM Product p
    INNER JOIN Sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id
    GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category
    ORDER BY total_sales DESC
    LIMIT ?
  )";

  try {
    using ConnType = sqlgen::SQLiteConn;
    ConnType conn("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto query =
        conn.prepare(select_from).transform([&](auto&& _stmt) {
          _stmt.bind_text(1, std::to_string(limit_count), SQLITE_STATIC);
          return _stmt.execute().transform([](auto&& _res) -> std::vector<TopProduct> {
            return sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(_res | transform(to_top_product));
          });
        }));
    return query.value_or(std::vector<TopProduct>());
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    return std::vector<TopProduct>();
  }
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace order_by_descending {

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int points;
};

}
}  // namespace order_by_descending
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates dropping a table if it exists.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
  auto table = sqlgen::make_table<Log>();
  auto query = sqlgen::DropTable{.table = table, .if_exists = true};
  return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query =
      "SELECT CAST(strftime('%w', sale_time) AS INT) AS weekday, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity "
      "FROM Sales "
      "GROUP BY weekday;";
  return conn->template read<std::vector<WeekdayStats>>>(query);
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

inline std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
  return sqlgen::update<Player>()
      .set(Player::score, sqlgen::columns().score + 10)
      .to_sql();
}
}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace project_time_tracking {

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
};

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> log_id;

  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> end_time;
};

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
  int day;
  int month;
  int year;
  int hour;
  int minute;
  int second;
  double duration_days;
};

std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
  using ConnType = sqlgen::SQLiteConn;
  auto conn = ConnType::make("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = conn->begin_transaction();

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT p.project_id, p.name,
           MIN(strftime('%d', t.start_time)) AS day,
           MIN(strftime('%m', t.start_time)) AS month,
           MIN(strftime('%Y', t.start_time)) AS year,

           MIN(strftime('%S', t.start_time)) AS second,

    FROM Project p
    INNER JOIN TimeLog t ON p.project_id = t.project_id
    GROUP BY p.project_id, strftime('%Y-%W', t.start_time)
  )";

  const auto res = session->execute(query);

  std::vector<TimeMetrics> metrics;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    TimeMetrics tm;
    tm.project_id = row.get<int>("project_id");
    tm.name = row.get<std::string>("name");

    tm.second = row.get<int>("second");
    tm.duration_days = row.get<double>("duration_days");
    metrics.push_back(std::move(tm));
  }

}

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace group_by_with_aggregations {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct Stats {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  int post_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_length;
};

std::vector<Stats> group_by_with_aggregations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<User, Post>(
      sqlgen::join<User, Post, sqlgen::JoinType::inner_join,
                       [](const auto& u, const auto& p) { return u.user_id == p.user_id; }),
      sqlgen::fields(
          sqlgen::field(User_.user_id).as("user_id"),
          sqlgen::field(User_.name).as("name"),
          sqlgen::agg(sqlgen::count(Post_.post_id)).as("post_count"),
          sqlgen::agg(sqlgen::avg(Post_.content.length())).as("avg_length")
      ),
      sqlgen::group_by(User_.user_id, User_.name)
  );

  return conn->execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& cursor) {
        std::vector<Stats> stats;
        while (cursor.next()) {
          Stats s;
          s.user_id = cursor.get<int>("user_id");
          s.name = cursor.get<std::string>("name");
          s.post_count = cursor.get<int>("post_count");
          s.avg_length = cursor.get<double>("avg_length");
          stats.push_back(std::move(s));
        }
        return stats;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<Stats>());
}
}  // namespace group_by_with_aggregations
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_table_as_select {

struct Source {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int value;
};

struct Target {
  int value;
};

inline auto create_table_as_select() {
    auto select_from = sqlgen::SelectFrom<Source>{
        .fields = {sqlgen::columns<Source>().value},
        .where = sqlgen::columns<Source>().value > 100
    };

    auto query = sqlgen::create_as<Target>(select_from, "Target", "", false, false, false);

    return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace create_table_as_select
```
</answer>
